design_philosophy.core_loop: First-person dungeon navigation with turn-based card-themed battles. Party management around protagonist Rooks and rotating elemental spirits, managing limited inventory and resources between town safe points.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity and permadeath mechanics. Any character death except elementals triggers game over. Limited save points in towns force risk/reward decisions about dungeon depth.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become the last Card Master, learning elemental spirit magic to prevent the resurrection of an ancient evil empress and restore peace to the war-torn land of Elemen.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Arcana commits fully to old-school dungeon crawling difficulty. Unlike contemporary RPGs, it treats any non-elemental character death as party-ending failure. It rejects hand-holding via limited forewarning of boss encounters, non-stackable inventory (42 slots max), save-point scarcity, and permanent dungeon linearity—once completed, dungeons are inaccessible, preventing grinding or backtracking. This creates genuine jeopardy.

difficulty.lives_system: No traditional lives. Single character death (except elementals) = game over. Elemental spirits can die and be rotated, but if protagonist Rooks falls while an elemental is dead, player is locked into failure state.
difficulty.checkpointing: Save points limited to town areas per level. Dungeons feature no mid-dungeon checkpoints. Players must navigate labyrinthine passages without saves.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player has agency in party composition and spirit rotation, but difficulty is largely imposed. Dungeon linearity prevents grinding weaker areas. Inventory limits (42 slots, no stacking) force tactical item management. Hidden boss encounters with little forewarning remove player ability to prepare.

progression.upgrade_system: Standardized level progression with no randomness in stat increases. Rooks gains access to high-level resurrection spells via experience thresholds. Four elemental spirits (Sylph/wind, Efrite/fire, Dao/earth, Marid/water) provide switching-based tactical variety within fixed party structure.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-driven progression gates dungeon access. Completion of one dungeon determines which dungeon is accessible next. No player choice in dungeon order.
replayability_hooks: Four distinct elemental spirits with different magical properties encourage experimentation with party compositions
Spirit rotation mid-battle provides tactical puzzle-solving within fixed mechanics
Card-based character and ability metaphor provides thematic consistency for replaying familiar mechanics

world_building_techniques: First-person perspective maintained during conversations and dungeon exploration, immersing player in world from character viewpoint
Card metaphor applied thematically: character death = torn card, magical abilities explained through card properties
Environmental narrative through dungeon design—labyrinthine passages with dead ends suggest a hostile, chaotic land
Backstory of 10-year civil war, Card Master persecution, and missing royal daughters provides narrative framing for player quest

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters are discovery-based environmental hazards rather than telegraphed set pieces. Bosses occupy specific dungeon tiles; players encounter them through exploration without forewarning. This creates jeopardy and forces engagement with unknown threats.
notable_bosses: Empress Rimsala (ancient sealed evil, resurrection is central plot goal—implied final boss)
Galneon (former court magician, villain orchestrating Rimsala's revival, hunted Card Masters)

faction_indicators: Card Masters—persecuted wizards hunted by Galneon; protagonist Rooks inherits their legacy
Knights of Lexford—dissolved military order sworn to King Wagnall; Rooks's father was among them
Galneon's regime—oppressive authority stretched across Elemen after civil war, actively eliminating Card Masters
danger_signals: Mysterious events occurring across the land herald approaching cataclysm
Unsealing and resurrection of Rimsala represents existential threat to Elemen
Hidden boss encounters in dungeons signal that Galneon's forces actively oppose the player
environmental_storytelling: Labyrinthine dungeon design reflects chaos and danger of post-war Elemen
Town safe points contrast with hostile dungeons, suggesting civilization under siege
Elemental spirit mechanics reflect Card Master magic tradition—four elements fundamental to world's metaphysical order
